items.title: Preset Event Priority Levels not transferring
items.description:
Reporter: J. Garcia

Date & Time: Consistent

Description:
There's a preset priority level for each event type. When that event is selected while creating a call, the preset level doesn't carry. It should obviously remain editable by the dispatcher, however in normal call creation that should carry over.

Where did this occur?
Any/every device used, desktop

Steps to Reproduce:
Create an event, select a high priority event type, it doesn't change on the editable screen or when you create it.

Expected Behavior:/
Any/every device used, desktop

Actual Behavior::
It stayed a level 3

Severity:
L...

items.title: Feature: Plot Map
items.description:
Requestor: J. Garcia

Summary:
Able to plot call locations on the map and create a route to it?

Problem to Solve:
More map featues

Workflow / Use Case:


Alternate Ideas:

comments.author: ryanpfister
comments.body: Commit origin/claude/brookhaven-cad-system-NK6yM

comments.author: ryanpfister
comments.body:
Call location pins

Every active/pending call is geocoded and plotted as a colored diamond pin
P1 calls = red, P2 = amber, P3+ = gray
The pin label shows the event type code (e.g. 1039/ASSTO), and the popup shows event number, type, priority, status, and address
Routing to assigned call

If an officer is signed in, assigned to a call, and has GPS active — a dashed cyan line is drawn from their pin to the call pin
The assigned call popup shows "★ MY CALL" to make it immediately identifiable
The "Get Directions" link opens Google Maps with the officer's GPS as the starting point; for dispatchers...

comments.author: ryanpfister
comments.body:
Geocode event addresses on map open using the existing /api/geocode Nominatim proxy, throttled and cached per address string
Plot event pins colored by priority (P1=red, P2=amber, others=dim) with event number, type, and address in the popup
Route polyline — if an officer is on duty and assigned to a call, draw a dashed green line from their GPS pin to that call's pin
Directions link — event popup opens Google Maps with saddr=officer GPS → daddr=event location when the viewer is an officer with GPS; otherwise just the event location
Fit bounds includes both officer pins and event pins

items.title: Feature: Officer / General View event from unit monitor
items.description:
Requestor: J. Garcia

Summary:
Allow accounts to click on the event from the unit monitor to get details about it, not just from the event monitor.

Problem to Solve:
Allow users to easily view events.

Workflow / Use Case:


Alternate Ideas:

comments.author: ryanpfister
comments.body: See commit claude/brookhaven-cad-system-NK6yM

comments.author: ryanpfister
comments.body:
licking an event number in the unit monitor now opens the full call detail modal. The implementation:

UnitRow — event number cell is a clickable <button> (with stopPropagation) when the unit has an active event; clicking the row itself still opens the status modal as before
DGroupSection — passes onEventClick through to each UnitRow
UnitStatusBoard — tracks selectedEventId locally and renders EventDetailModal when set; canEdit/canNote are derived from the logged-in user's role so officers get read-only view while dispatchers can edit
Works in all tab configurations — units-only, split view, a...

comments.author: ryanpfister
comments.body:
UnitRow — make event number cell a clickable button calling onEventClick(eventId)
DGroupSection — pass onEventClick through
UnitStatusBoard — add selectedEventId state, render EventDetailModal

items.title: Feature: Make a tab to view both the event and unit monitor side by side.
items.description:
Requestor: J. Garcia

Summary:
I want a view to see both pages side by side

Problem to Solve:
Allow units to see both.

Workflow / Use Case:


Alternate Ideas:

comments.author: ryanpfister
comments.body:
Commit claude/brookhaven-cad-system-NK6yM should fix this issue.

The DispatchPage already has a split-pane layout — the OfficerPage uses tabs and has no equivalent. The fix is to add a SPLIT option to the OfficerPage tab bar that shows events and units simultaneously. Simple one-file change.

Done. A SPLIT tab now appears in the officer page tab bar between UNITS and MAP. Clicking it shows the event board on the left half and the unit board on the right half simultaneously — the same layout the dispatch page uses. Clicking any other tab returns to single-pane view.

items.title: Multiple Units
items.description:
If multiple units are signed in as the same callsign (for example 2 201's) dont allow it, allow the officer who is signing in to kick the other 201 off, but give them an option in case they forgot to sign out.

Also sometimes we have two units riding in 1 car, allow this to happen as well.

items.title: Create noted with status's
items.description:
General -
Add the ability to create notes with statuses, mainly when not assigned to a call, so if someone says they are on a personal or taking a 12 (return to town hall) per 104, you can put "PER 104" and its visible on the unit monitor.

items.title: Change Status
items.description:
General-
Change statuses to Available (green), assigned or dispatched (yellow), enroute (blue), on scene (orange), unavailable (white), 10-18 (meal) (teal), 10-12 (returning to town hall) (brown), out of service (purple), and needs assistance (red)
